content.full_text:
February 21, 1990

Jon Rothman
Legal Division
FPPC
428 J Street #800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Rothman:

I am writing in response to our telephone conversation of February 16th. I would like to reiterate the details of our conversation and obtain a written opinion from your office.

A constituent approached Senator Hart and offered to have his family host a fundraising event to benefit the Senator’s re-election campaign. This family would like to hold this event at a ranch in the Santa Monica Mountains which is often rented for events. There is absolutely no relationship between the ranch and the Senator’s campaign; the family has both personal and business relationships with the ranch owner and has hosted charitable events with his help in the past. If the ranch owner enters into a contract with our constituent family and the family then hosts the event, is the campaign contribution from the family?

It is my understanding that the ranch owner is intending to charge approximately $8.50 per person for the food and not charge for the use of his facility. His posted price list includes an $8.00 per person charge for use of the facility. The host family will ask Senator Hart’s campaign to pay for the food and would donate the use of the facility.

On a slightly different subject, in Friday’s conversation, you informed me that if the ranch owner is the controlling director of the corporation, there is only one contribution possible from both the owner and the corporation. However, if other owners wished to make contributions, they could do so. For instance, if the ranch owner’s wife or mother had an interest in the business they could also make "in kind" contributions to the campaign.

Jon Rothman  
Legal Division  
FPPC  
February 21, 1990  
Page 2

If you need additional information or clarification regarding my question, or if I misunderstood Friday's conversation in any way, please do not hesitate to contact me. Please respond to me by mail at 4260 Old Topanga Canyon Road; I can be reached by phone at home at (818)703-1420.

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this.

Sincerely,

Elaine Miller
